title: WIP Recursively stop a pod.
body:
Before, there existed an edge case where a container in a pod stop would fail to stop, which would allow it to stop after the pod infra container. This breaks the invariant that the infra container should be running while any container is running. fix this by recursively stopping in a pod.

Signed-off-by: Peter Hunt <pehunt@redhat.com>

@mheon PTAL the first version. I'm not sure I'm happy yet because the pod version of the reproducer of #2577 still fails to stop (I can kill the memcache container, but then I can't stop the infra container with a 'pod stop'). It does, however, keep the infra container running if memcache can't be stopped, which maintains the invariant.

@haircommander Didn't we figure out that this was actually a rootless issue?

I'm not opposed to these patches though, just don't think we have an actual race here

Isn't there a simpler way to do this. By stopping all of the containers first and then stopping the infracontainer?

BTW:In a shared PID Namespace as soon as the infracontainer stops all of the other containers will die.

We can't guaranteed shared PID namespace, though.

I don't actually know if this one is necessary. I think the main issue we ran into was permissions, not ordering.
Yeah I'm also unconvinced it's necessary
I'll keep the branch around but I'm inclined to not add such complex code for a problem that isn't really supposed to be a problem
I was just adding info on the PID Namespace, not saying it solves all issues.  I would prefer to stay away from the complexity of this code also.
